goal: set_image[T, U](period_dividing_set[T](f, n), h).contains(y) = period_dividing_set[U](g, n).contains(y)
set_image[T, U](period_dividing_set[T](f, n), h) = period_dividing_set[U](g, n)
proof: let w0: U satisfy { w0 ∈ set_image[T, U](period_dividing_set[T](f, n), h) != w0 ∈ period_dividing_set[U](g, n) }

goal: exists(k0: T) { prime_period_set[T](f, n).contains(k0) and y = h(k0) }
is_conjugacy[T, U](h, k, f, g) = (is_semiconjugacy[T, U](h, f, g) and forall(x0: T) { k(h(x0)) = x0 } and forall(x1: U) { h(k(x1)) = x1 })
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1, x1: T0 -> T0, x2: T1 -> T1, x3: T1 -> T0) { (is_semiconjugacy[T0, T1](x0, x1, x2) and forall(x4: T0) { x3(x0(x4)) = x4 } and forall(x5: T1) { x0(x3(x5)) = x5 }) = is_conjugacy[T0, T1](x0, x3, x1, x2) }[T, U](h, f, g, k)
function[T0, T1](x0: T1 -> T0, x1: T0 -> T1) { forall(x2: T0) { x0(x1(x2)) = x2 } = lib(functions).is_left_inverse_fn[T0, T1](x1, x0) }[U, T](h, k)
function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1, x1: T1 -> T0, x2: T0) { not lib(functions).is_left_inverse_fn[T0, T1](x0, x1) or x2 = x1(x0(x2)) }[U, T](k, h, w)
function(x0: U) { h(k(x0)) = x0 }(y)

goal: set_image[T, U](prime_period_set[T](f, n), h).contains(y) = prime_period_set[U](g, n).contains(y)
set_image[T, U](prime_period_set[T](f, n), h) = prime_period_set[U](g, n)
proof: let w0: U satisfy { w0 ∈ set_image[T, U](prime_period_set[T](f, n), h) != w0 ∈ prime_period_set[U](g, n) }
